title: Alianza F.C. (Colombia)

infobox.value: is a Colombian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team based in [Valledupar](/wiki/Valledupar "Valledupar"), [Cesar Department](/wiki/Cesar_Department "Cesar Department"), that currently plays in the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A"). The club was founded on 16 January 2024 and plays its home games at the [Armando Maestre Pavajeau](/wiki/Estadio_Armando_Maestre_Pavajeau "Estadio Armando Maestre Pavajeau") stadium.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Alianza F.C. (Colombia)](/w/index.php?title=Alianza_F.C._(Colombia)&redirect=no "Alianza F.C. (Colombia)"))

Association football club in Colombia

This article is about the football club based in Valledupar, Colombia. For the Salvadoran football club, see [Alianza F.C. (El Salvador)](/wiki/Alianza_F.C._(El_Salvador) "Alianza F.C. (El Salvador)"). For the Panamanian football club, see [Alianza F.C. (Panama)](/wiki/Alianza_F.C._(Panama) "Alianza F.C. (Panama)").

Football club

Alianza Valledupar

**Alianza Fútbol Club** is a Colombian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team based in [Valledupar](/wiki/Valledupar "Valledupar"), [Cesar Department](/wiki/Cesar_Department "Cesar Department"), that currently plays in the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A"). The club was founded on 16 January 2024 and plays its home games at the [Armando Maestre Pavajeau](/wiki/Estadio_Armando_Maestre_Pavajeau "Estadio Armando Maestre Pavajeau") stadium.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alianza_Valledupar_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Further information: [Alianza Petrolera F.C.](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera_F.C. "Alianza Petrolera F.C.")

Alianza F.C. was founded by the owner and chairman of [Alianza Petrolera](/wiki/Alianza_Petrolera_F.C. "Alianza Petrolera F.C."), Carlos Orlando Ferreira, who relocated the club from [Barrancabermeja](/wiki/Barrancabermeja "Barrancabermeja"), [Santander Department](/wiki/Santander_Department "Santander Department") after being offered financial support by the newly elected authorities and private enterprises from [Valledupar](/wiki/Valledupar "Valledupar") and the [Cesar Department](/wiki/Cesar_Department "Cesar Department").

Whilst Alianza Petrolera came from an eighth-place finish in the aggregate table of the [2023 Categoría Primera A season](/wiki/2023_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2023 Categoría Primera A season") that qualified them for their first international competition, the [2024 Copa Sudamericana](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana "2024 Copa Sudamericana"), by early January 2024 issues regarding financial support as well as the fact that [Estadio Daniel Villa Zapata](/wiki/Estadio_Daniel_Villa_Zapata "Estadio Daniel Villa Zapata") in Barrancabermeja was not suitable to host international games came up, along with an interest from Valledupar for the club to play their home matches there.[[1]](#cite_note-1) On 16 January 2024 Alianza Petrolera officially confirmed their departure from Barrancabermeja due to the lack of financial support from local authorities while also dropping the word *Petrolera* from their name and the yellow and black colors that identified them.[[2]](#cite_note-2) That same day, *Alianza Fútbol Club* was presented at a press conference held in Valledupar, where it was confirmed that the club would play under a new crest and would adopt as new colors those representative of the city.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The club played its first match on 21 January 2024, losing 3–1 to [Atlético Nacional](/wiki/Atl%C3%A9tico_Nacional "Atlético Nacional") for the first round of the [2024 Apertura](/wiki/2024_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season#Torneo_Apertura "2024 Categoría Primera A season") tournament.[[4]](#cite_note-4) On 6 March Alianza played their first international match, defeating [América de Cali](/wiki/Am%C3%A9rica_de_Cali "América de Cali") 2–1 in the [first stage](/wiki/2024_Copa_Sudamericana_first_stage "2024 Copa Sudamericana first stage") of the Copa Sudamericana to advance to the group stage of the competition.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Despite staging an upset against [Cruzeiro](/wiki/Esporte_Clube_Cruzeiro "Esporte Clube Cruzeiro") with a 3–3 draw at [Mineirão](/wiki/Mineir%C3%A3o "Mineirão") in their second group stage match,[[6]](#cite_note-6) Alianza failed to make any further progress in the competition.

## Stadium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Alianza_Valledupar_F.C.&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Stadium")]

Further information: [Estadio Armando Maestre Pavajeau](/wiki/Estadio_Armando_Maestre_Pavajeau "Estadio Armando Maestre Pavajeau")

Alianza F.C. play their home matches at [Estadio Armando Maestre Pavajeau](/wiki/Estadio_Armando_Maestre_Pavajeau "Estadio Armando Maestre Pavajeau") in Valledupar, built in 1974 and renovated between 2013 and 2015 to host the [2015 South American U-15 Championship](/wiki/2015_South_American_U-15_Championship "2015 South American U-15 Championship"). The stadium, with a capacity of 11,000 spectators, served previously as home stadium for former [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B") club [Valledupar F.C.](/wiki/Valledupar_F.C. "Valledupar F.C."), which folded in June 2023.
